TREE DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAY IN WEST HARLINGEN.
FALLEN TREE DAMAGED A CAR ON DIAZ STREET IN SAN BENITO. PHOTO SUBMITTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NOAA BUOY STATION 42020 MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 47 MPH OR 40.8 KNOTS AT 7:30 AM CDT.
FALFURRIAS POLICE DEPT. REPORTED DOWNED POWER LINES IN FALFURRIAS.
THE SANTA ANA NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE REPORTED A LARGE TREE DOWN ON THE ROAD, TAKING SOME POWER LINES DOWN WITH IT AROUND 5:00 AM.
PUBLIC REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL 2.7 MILES SOUTH OF ALAMO AT 5:00 AM
PUBLIC REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL 2.7 MILES SOUTH OF ALAMO AT 5:00 AM.
DOWNED TREE IN SOUTH ALAMO. REPORT RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
PUBLIC ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZE HAIL FALLING IN THE CITY OF ALAMO AT 5:14 AM VIA SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT.
HIDALGO COUNTY EM REPORTS SEVERAL DOWNED TREES AND FENCES IN THE CITY OF NORTH ALAMO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
HIDALGO COUNTY EM REPORTS A ROOF BLOWN OFF A MOBILE HOME IN NORTH ALAMO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
MCCOOK CO-OP OBSERVER REPORTED SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS AND STREET SIGNS BLOWN DOWN IN THE TOWN OF MCCOOK.
MCCOOK CO-OP OBSERVER REPORTED DOWNED POWER LINES 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF THE TOWN OF MCCOOK.
STRUTURAL DAMAGE TO MOBILE HOME, INCLUDING THE ROOF BEING TORN OFF AND PARTIAL WALL COLLAPSE.
MEDIA REPORTING POLICE CLOSING THE SOLIS ROAD EXIT ALONG WESTBOUND EXPRESSWAY 83 DUE TO FLOODING.

WILLACY COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS DAMAGE TO 8 UTILITY POLES AND 20 TO 30 MESQUITE TREES ON THE FRONTAGE ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CONLEY AND 77. DAMAGE TO UTILITY POLES A COMBINATION OF BEING REMOVED FROM THE GROUND AND BROKEN IN HALF. BARK PEELED OFF SOME OF THE MESQUITE TREES. WIDTH OF DAMAGE TO POLES AND TREES ONE HALF MILE LONG. ALSO, GRASS OF ONE AND A HALF FEET TALL APPEARS SPUN AROUND IN PLACES.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN ACROSS LASARA.
NWS STORM SURVEY ESTIMATED PEAK STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 65 TO 75 MPH HAD IMPACTED THE MONTE ALTO AREA RESULTING IN SPORADIC STRUCTURAL AND TREE DAMAGE.

UPDATED UP TO THREE UNANCHORED TRAILER HOMES DAMAGED AND NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FM 2812 AND FM 493.TWO INDIVIDUAL INSIDE BUT NO DETAILS ON ANY CASUALTIES.UPDATED BY HIDALGO COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT.
UNANCHORED TRAILER HOME ROLLED OVER AT INTERSECTION OF FM 2812 AND FM 493. FAMILY INSIDE BUT NO DETAILS ON ANY CASUALTIES.
ONE TREE BLOWN DOWN IN DONNA ON CUMMINGS ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED.
PORCH OVERHANG TORN OFF THE BACK OF A HOME IN SOUTHWEST ALAMO THIS PAST MONDAY EVENING.
HOMES BEING EVACUATED IN THE MONTE ALTO AREA DUE TO FLOODING OF UNKNOWN DEPTH. 5 TO 6 INCHES OF RAINFALL ESTAIMTED. EVENT ONGOING AT TIME OF REPORT WITH UPDATES EXPECTED LATER.
MULTIPLE ROADS CLOSED IN LASARA DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
MULTIPLE ROADS CLOSED IN THE MERCEDES AREA DUE TO FLOOD WATERS, INCLUDING THE FRONTAGE ROADS AND EXITS ALONG INTERSTATE 2.
MULTIPLE ONGOING WATER RESCUES IN THE LASARA AREA WITH WATER DEPTH OF IN EXCESS OF 4 FEET.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORT MULIPLE WATER RESCUES ONGOING IN THE MONTE ALTO AREA. 3 TO 4 FEET OF WATER DEPTH IN THE MONTE ALTO AREA.
MULTIPLE ROADS CLOSED IN LA FERIA
MULTIPLE ROADS CLOSED.
HARLINGEN POLICE REPORTED NUMEROUS ROADS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING INCLUDING WILSON ROAD AND INTERSTATE 69E FRONTAGE ROAD.
CAMERON COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED FLOODING ON INTERSTATE 2 AND THE FRONTAGE ROAD BETWEEN LA FERIA AND HARLINGEN.
WATER ENTERING HOMES AND NUMEROUS ROAD CLOSURES DUE TO FLASH FLOODING NEAR LYFORD.
PHOTOS RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

MULTIPLE STALLED OUT AND ABANDONED VEHICLES ON FLOODED ROADS; BOTH NORTHBOUND AND SOUTHBOUND FRONTAGE ROADS ALONG I-69E IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODED WATERS FROM HARLINGEN TO COMBES; WATER ENTERING BUSINESSES AND MOBILE HOMES ALONG FRONTAGE ROAD.
DOZENS OF PEOPLE EVACUATED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
SEVERAL CARS STALLED OUT ON FLOODED ROADS. WATER LEVELS UP TO CAR HOODS IN SOME LOCATIONS.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON ROAD. ALSO, LARGE BRANCHES DOWN NEARBY.
LARGE TREE DOWN ON ROAD. ALSO, LARGE BRANCHES DOWN NEARBY.
WATER REPORTED UP TO HOME FOUNDATIONS AND IN SOME HOMES IN THIS AREA.
MULTIPLE CLOSED ROADS DUE TO FLOOD WATERS IN SANTA ROSA. COOP OBSERVER REPORTED 15.20 INCHES OF RAIN HAD FALLEN THROUGH THE NIGHT.

BEACH PATROL AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED THAT A WATERSPOUT MOVED ASHORE OFF OF THE GULF OF MEXICO AT BEACH ACCESS 3 ON SOUTH PADRE ISLAND. IT DISPLACED BEACH UMBRELLAS AND TENTS. TIME ESTIMATED AT 3:15 PM LOCAL TIME.

BEACH PATROL AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED THAT A WATERSPOUT MOVED ASHORE OFF OF THE GULF OF MEXICO NEAR BEACH ACCESS 10 ON SOUTH PADRE ISLAND. IT DISPLACED BEACH UMBRELLAS AND TENTS. TIME ESTIMATED AT 3:15 PM LOCAL TIME.

TWO ANCHORED DEER BLINDS WERE BLOWN ONTO THE HIGHWAY FROM A STORE DISPLAY NEARBY, JUST SOUTH OF THE BRIDGE BETWEEN FALCON LAKE ESTATES AND THE CITY OF ZAPATA. ESTIMATED TIME OF THE STRONGEST WINDS WAS BETWEEN 509 AND 513 PM. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS AROUND 60 MPH.

INITIAL REPORT OF ONE ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOME IN RAYMONDVILLE, AND MANY CALLS OF POWER POLES AND TREES DOWN BETWEEN RAYMONDVILLE AND LYFORD. PRELIMINARY TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR WAS BETWEEN 542 AND 602 PM.
DAUGHTER OF FORMER NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL ALONG NORTH 17TH STREET IN MCALLEN.
SEVERAL PORTIONS OF A FENCE WERE BLOWN DOWN AT THE CHABAD OF THE RGV IN MCALLEN. DAMAGE TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SOUTH PADRE ISLAND POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTS DOWNED POWERLINES IN BETWEEN THE PARALLELING PADRE BLVD AND LAGUNA BLVD ON SOUTH PADRE ISLAND. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TCOON STATION AT BRAZOS SANTIAGO PASS MEASURED 56-KNOT WIND GUST.
